### Quick Start: Manaflow AI Cloud Development Workflow

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

This guide walks through the essential steps to get started with Manaflow AI's cloud development environment. It covers setting the API key, starting the daemon, creating and managing workspaces, launching the cloud VM, interacting with web elements using AI-friendly references, saving and resuming state, and stopping the environment.

```bash
# 1. Set your API key
export MORPH_API_KEY="your-api-key"

# 2. Start the daemon
cmux daemon start

# 3. Create a workspace
cmux create my-app --template=node

# 4. Get your workspace ID
cmux list
# Find your workspace ID (e.g., ws_abc123)

# 5. Start the Morph Cloud VM
cmux computer start -w ws_abc123
# Output shows URLs for VS Code, VNC, and App

# 6. Open a website in the cloud browser
cmux computer open "https://example.com" -w ws_abc123

# 7. Get interactive elements (THE KEY FEATURE!)
cmux computer snapshot -i -w ws_abc123
# Output:
# @e1: link "More information..."

# 8. Interact with elements using refs
cmux computer click @e1 -w ws_abc123

# 9. Take a screenshot to verify
cmux computer screenshot --output=result.png -w ws_abc123

# 10. Save state for later
cmux computer save --name=my-checkpoint -w ws_abc123

# 11. Stop when done
cmux computer stop -w ws_abc123

# 12. Later, resume exactly where you left off
cmux computer start --from=my-checkpoint -w ws_abc123
# Browser state, processes - everything restored!
```

--------------------------------

### Quick Test: Build, Login, and Start VM

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/README.md

This sequence of commands guides through the initial setup for testing. It involves building the cmux-devbox, logging in, and starting a new virtual machine.

```bash
cd packages/cmux-devbox
make build
./bin/cmux-devbox login
./bin/cmux-devbox start
```

--------------------------------

### Quick Start: Managing Cloud VMs with cmux

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/README.md

A comprehensive quick start guide for using the cmux devbox CLI. It covers authentication, VM creation and syncing, accessing VMs via VS Code, SSH, or VNC, executing commands, managing VM lifecycle (pause, resume, delete), and listing VMs.

```bash
# 1. Login
cmux auth login

# 2. Create a VM
cmux start                     # Creates VM, returns ID (e.g., cmux_abc123)
cmux start ./my-project        # Creates VM and syncs directory

# 3. Access the VM
cmux code cmux_abc123           # Open VS Code in browser
cmux ssh cmux_abc123            # SSH into VM
cmux vnc cmux_abc123            # Open VNC desktop in browser

# 4. Work with the VM
cmux exec cmux_abc123 "npm install"    # Run commands
cmux sync cmux_abc123 ./my-project     # Sync files to VM

# 5. Manage VM lifecycle
cmux pause cmux_abc123          # Pause (preserves state, saves cost)
cmux resume cmux_abc123         # Resume paused VM
cmux delete cmux_abc123         # Delete VM permanently

# 6. List VMs
cmux ls                        # List all your VMs
```

--------------------------------

### Manual Worker Daemon Setup on VM

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/README.md

Step-by-step instructions to manually install and start the browser automation worker daemon on a VM if it's not already set up. Involves installing agent-browser, uploading the worker script, and starting the daemon.

```bash
# 1. Install agent-browser
./bin/cmux-devbox exec <id> "npm install -g agent-browser"

# 2. Upload the worker daemon script
cat packages/cmux-devbox/worker/server.js | base64 | tr -d '\n' > /tmp/worker_b64.txt
B64=$(cat /tmp/worker_b64.txt)
./bin/cmux-devbox exec <id> "echo '$B64' | base64 -d > /usr/local/bin/cmux-devbox-worker && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/cmux-devbox-worker"

# 3. Create token directory and start worker
./bin/cmux-devbox exec <id> "mkdir -p /var/run/cmux"
./bin/cmux-devbox exec <id> "nohup node /usr/local/bin/cmux-devbox-worker > /var/log/cmux-devbox-worker.log 2>&1 &"

# 4. Verify worker is running
./bin/cmux-devbox exec <id> "curl -s http://localhost:39377/health"
# Output: {"status":"ok"}

# 5. Get the auth token
./bin/cmux-devbox exec <id> "cat /var/run/cmux/worker-token"
```

--------------------------------

### Manual Testing Walkthrough with Manaflow AI

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

A step-by-step guide for manual testing within the Manaflow AI environment, covering setup, service management, and browser interactions.

```bash
# 1. Build and prepare
cd dba # Note: the package directory is still named 'dba'
make build
export PATH="$PWD/bin:$PATH"
export MORPH_API_KEY="your-api-key"

# 2. Start the daemon
cmux daemon start

# 3. Create a test workspace
cmux create test-manual --template=node
cmux list
# Note the workspace ID (e.g., ws_9fce9a7e)
export WS_ID=ws_9fce9a7e  # Replace with your actual ID

# 4. Start the Morph VM
cmux computer start -w $WS_ID

# 5. Test browser navigation
cmux computer open "https://example.com" -w $WS_ID
cmux computer get url -w $WS_ID
cmux computer get title -w $WS_ID

# 6. Test element discovery
cmux computer snapshot -i -w $WS_ID

# 7. Test element interaction
cmux computer click @e1 -w $WS_ID

# 8. Test screenshot
cmux computer screenshot --output=/tmp/test.png -w $WS_ID
ls -la /tmp/test.png

# 9. Clean up
cmux computer stop -w $WS_ID
cmux destroy test-manual --force
cmux daemon stop

echo "All tests passed!"
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshooting: VM Won't Start

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Provides steps to troubleshoot issues with the VM not starting, including checking API key configuration and daemon status.

```bash
# Check if MORPH_API_KEY is set
echo $MORPH_API_KEY

# Verify API connection
cmux daemon status
```

--------------------------------

### Quick Start Commands

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cloudrouter/README.md

Basic commands to start a sandbox, access it via VS Code or terminal, and run commands.

```APIDOC
## Quick Start Commands

### Create a sandbox from the current directory
```bash
cloudrouter start .
```

### Open VS Code in the browser
```bash
cloudrouter code <id>
```

### Get a terminal
```bash
cloudrouter pty <id>
```

### Run a command in the sandbox
```bash
cloudrouter exec <id> "<command>"
```

### Open VNC desktop
```bash
cloudrouter vnc <id>
```

### Open Jupyter Lab
```bash
cloudrouter jupyter <id>
```
```

--------------------------------

### Installation and Authentication

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cloudrouter/README.md

Instructions for installing Cloudrouter as a skill or standalone CLI, and how to authenticate.

```APIDOC
## Installation and Authentication

### Install as a skill
```bash
npx skills add manaflow-ai/cloudrouter
```

### Install as a standalone CLI
```bash
npm install -g @manaflow-ai/cloudrouter
```

### Authenticate
```bash
cloudrouter login
```

### Build from source
```bash
cd packages/cloudrouter
make build-dev
make install-dev
```
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Build cmux-devbox-cli

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

This snippet shows how to clone the cmux-devbox-cli repository, navigate into the directory, build the CLI using make, and optionally add the binary to your system's PATH for easier access. It concludes with a command to verify the installation.

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/anthropics/manaflow-ai/manaflow.git
cd manaflow/cmux-devbox-cli/dba

# Build the CLI
make build

# Add to PATH (optional)
export PATH="$PWD/bin:$PATH"

# Verify installation
./bin/cmux-devbox --help
```

--------------------------------

### Install cmux devbox CLI using Go install

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/internal/README.md

Installs the cmux devbox CLI to a user-defined binary directory using `go install`. It also demonstrates how to add this directory to the system's PATH for direct command execution.

```bash
cd packages/cmux-devboxGOBIN="$HOME/.local/bin" go install ./cmd/cmux-devbox
export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
```

--------------------------------

### Run cmux application

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux/README.md

Starts the cmux application. This command initiates the local Convex backend, starts the web server, and opens the application in your browser.

```bash
cmux
```

--------------------------------

### Common Workflow: Run Commands Remotely with cmux

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/npm/cmux/AGENTS.md

Example workflow showing how to execute commands on a remote VM using the `cmux exec` command. Useful for tasks like installing dependencies or starting development servers.

```bash
cmux exec cmux_abc123 "npm install"
cmux exec cmux_abc123 "npm run dev"
```

--------------------------------

### Start and Access CloudRouter Sandboxes

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cloudrouter/README.md

Demonstrates basic CloudRouter commands for starting a sandbox from the current directory, accessing it via VS Code in the browser, or opening an interactive terminal (pty).

```bash
# Create a sandbox from the current directory
cloudrouter start .

# Open VS Code in the browser
cloudrouter code cr_abc123

# Or get a terminal
cloudrouter pty cr_abc123
```

--------------------------------

### Create Manaflow Project with Template

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Initialize a new project using a predefined template like Node.js or Python. You can also clone a project from a Git repository and install additional packages.

```bash
cmux create my-app --template=node --clone=https://github.com/org/repo
cmux create my-app --template=python --packages=pytorch,numpy
```

--------------------------------

### Install cmux devbox CLI using Makefile

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/internal/README.md

Installs the cmux devbox CLI by building the binary and copying it to the system's PATH. This method is suitable for system-wide availability.

```bash
cd packages/cmux-devbox
make build
sudo make install
```

--------------------------------

### Quick Start Commands for PR Review Evals (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/www/scripts/pr-review/evals/README.md

Provides essential commands to get started with the PR review evaluation framework. These include syncing the dataset, inspecting a specific PR, and running an evaluation with a chosen strategy.

```bash
# 1. Sync all PRs from the dataset (fetches diffs, metadata)
bun run apps/www/scripts/pr-review/evals/sync-dataset.ts

# 2. Inspect a PR
bun run apps/www/scripts/pr-review/evals/inspect.ts <pr-id>

# 3. Run eval on a PR with a strategy
bun run apps/www/scripts/pr-review/evals/run-eval.ts <pr-id> [strategy-id]
```

--------------------------------

### Run Python Script with uv (Python)

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/scripts/hello-morph/docker-buildkit-example/README.md

This snippet uses the 'uv run' command to execute the docker-buildkit_setup.py script. 'uv' is a fast Python package installer and virtual environment manager that automatically handles dependencies.

```python
uv run docker-buildkit_setup.py
```

--------------------------------

### Start and Sync Sandbox from Local Directory

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/skills/cmux/SKILL.md

Creates a new cloud sandbox and synchronizes the specified local directory into it. The '.' shorthand refers to the current directory. This is the recommended method for setting up a development environment.

```bash
cmux start ./my-project
cmux start .
cmux start -o .
cmux start --git user/repo
cmux start --docker
```

--------------------------------

### Use Cloudrouter Sandbox CLI

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cloudrouter/template/README.md

Instructions for installing and using the Cloudrouter CLI to manage sandboxes. This includes creating, accessing VNC, and opening VSCode for a sandbox instance.

```bash
# Install CLI
cd packages/cloudrouter
make build-dev && make install-dev

# Create sandbox
cloudrouter start --name test -t <team>

# Open VNC (Chrome opens automatically)
cloudrouter vnc <id>

# Open VSCode
cloudrouter code <id>
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Build cmux Devbox CLI

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/README.md

Instructions to navigate to the cmux-devbox package directory, build the CLI, and view its help information. This is a prerequisite for using the cmux devbox CLI.

```bash
cd packages/cmux-devbox
make build
./bin/cmux-devbox --help
```

--------------------------------

### Install cmux using npm

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux/README.md

Installs the cmux package globally using npm. This command is used to set up the cmux application on your system for command-line usage.

```bash
npm install -g cmux
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshooting: Element Not Found

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Guides users on how to resolve 'element not found' errors, including refreshing stale refs, checking visibility, and using alternative selectors.

```bash
# Refs may be stale - refresh them
cmux computer snapshot -i -w $WS_ID

# Check if element is visible
cmux computer is visible @e1 -w $WS_ID

# Try using CSS selector instead
cmux computer click "#submit-btn" -w $WS_ID

# Or use text selector
cmux computer click "text=Submit" -w $WS_ID
```

--------------------------------

### VM Lifecycle Management

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/npm/cmux/AGENTS.md

Commands for creating, listing, starting, pausing, resuming, and deleting virtual machines.

```APIDOC
## VM Lifecycle Management

### Description
Commands to manage the lifecycle of your cloud development virtual machines.

### Commands
- `cmux start [path]`: Creates a new VM. Optionally, it can sync a local directory to the VM upon creation. Returns the VM ID.
- `cmux ls`: Lists all available VMs associated with the current user.
- `cmux status <id>`: Displays detailed information about a specific VM, including its URLs.
- `cmux pause <id>`: Pauses a running VM. This preserves the VM's state and stops incurring compute costs.
- `cmux resume <id>`: Resumes a previously paused VM.
- `cmux delete <id>`: Permanently deletes a VM and all its associated data.
```

--------------------------------

### Start and Provision Sandbox Environment

Source: https://context7.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/llms.txt

Creates a new sandbox instance with repository cloning and environment configuration. Supports cloud-based workspaces and optional pre-configured environment IDs.

```typescript
const response = await fetch('/api/sandboxes/start', {
  method: 'POST',
  headers: {
    'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    'Authorization': `Bearer ${accessToken}`
  },
  body: JSON.stringify({
    teamSlugOrId: 'my-team',
    repoUrl: 'https://github.com/owner/repo',
    branch: 'main',
    newBranch: 'feature/new-feature',
    ttlSeconds: 3600,
    taskRunId: 'task_abc123',
    taskRunJwt: 'jwt-token-here',
    isCloudWorkspace: true,
    depth: 1,
    environmentId: 'env_xyz789',
    metadata: {
      purpose: 'feature-development'
    }
  })
});
```

--------------------------------

### Common Workflow: Browser Automation with cmux

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/npm/cmux/AGENTS.md

Example workflow illustrating browser automation on a remote VM. This includes opening a URL, taking a snapshot to identify elements, and clicking a specific element.

```bash
cmux computer open cmux_abc123 "https://localhost:3000"
cmux computer snapshot cmux_abc123   # See clickable elements
cmux computer click cmux_abc123 @e1  # Click first element
```

--------------------------------

### Browser Automation Workflows

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/skills/cmux/SKILL.md

Examples of common browser automation tasks like logging in and scraping data.

```APIDOC
## Browser Automation Workflows

### Description
Examples demonstrating how to automate common browser tasks such as logging into websites and scraping data using the Manaflow AI CLI.

### Method
CLI Commands

### Endpoints
`cmux computer <command> <sandbox_id> [arguments]`

### Parameters
#### Command Arguments
- **open** (string) - Required - Opens a given URL in the sandbox's browser.
- **snapshot** - Required - Takes a snapshot of the current browser DOM, returning element references.
- **screenshot** (string) - Optional - Takes a screenshot of the browser and saves it to a file. Requires a filename.

### Request Example (Login)
```bash
cmux computer open cmux_abc123 "https://example.com/login"
cmux computer snapshot cmux_abc123
# Output might be: @e1 [input] Email, @e2 [input] Password, @e3 [button] Sign In
cmux computer fill cmux_abc123 @e1 "user@example.com"
cmux computer fill cmux_abc123 @e2 "password123"
cmux computer click cmux_abc123 @e3
cmux computer screenshot cmux_abc123 login_success.png
```

### Request Example (Scrape Data)
```bash
cmux computer open cmux_abc123 "https://example.com/data"
cmux computer snapshot cmux_abc123
# Process the snapshot output to extract desired data
cmux computer screenshot cmux_abc123 data_page.png
```

### Response
#### Success Response
- **snapshot**: Returns a list of element references with their types and descriptions.
- **screenshot**: Saves an image file.

#### Response Example (Snapshot)
```
@e1 [input] Email
@e2 [input] Password
@e3 [button] Sign In
```
```

--------------------------------

### Fish Autocompletion Setup for cmux

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/README.md

Instructions for setting up autocompletion for the cmux devbox CLI in the Fish shell. This involves sourcing the completion script or saving it to the Fish completions directory.

```bash
cmux completion fish | source
```

```bash
cmux completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/cmux.fish
```

--------------------------------

### Managing Multiple VMs with Manaflow

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/README.md

Illustrates how to start, manage, and list multiple virtual machines for different development tasks using the Manaflow CLI.

```bash
# Create multiple VMs for different tasks
cmux start ./frontend    # → cmux_frontend1
cmux start ./backend     # → cmux_backend1

# Work on them independently
cmux code cmux_frontend1
cmux code cmux_backend1

# List all
cmux ls
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Workspace Services with cmux

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Commands to manage services within a workspace. This includes starting, stopping, listing, viewing logs, and restarting services.

```bash
# Start all services
cmux up -w $WS_ID           
# Start specific services
cmux up web api -w $WS_ID   

# Stop services
cmux down [services...]

# List running services
cmux ps

# View service logs
cmux logs -w $WS_ID
cmux logs web -f -w $WS_ID  # Follow logs

# Restart services
cmux restart [services...]
```

--------------------------------

### Manage Sandboxes with cmux

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/skills/cmux/SKILL.md

Commands for creating, starting, and managing sandboxes. Includes opening VS Code, accessing the terminal, and transferring files. Requires a project path or sandbox ID.

```bash
cmux start ./my-project        # Creates sandbox, uploads files
cmux code cmux_abc123          # Open VS Code
cmux pty cmux_abc123           # Open terminal to run commands (e.g. npm install && npm run dev)
cmux upload cmux_abc123 ./my-project     # Push local files to sandbox
cmux download cmux_abc123 ./output       # Pull files from sandbox to local
```

--------------------------------

### Common Workflow: Sync Files with cmux

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/npm/cmux/AGENTS.md

Example workflows for synchronizing files between your local machine and a remote VM. Demonstrates pushing the current directory to the VM and pulling a build output directory from the VM.

```bash
cmux sync cmux_abc123 .              # Push current dir to VM
cmux sync cmux_abc123 ./dist --pull  # Pull build output from VM
```

--------------------------------

### Bash Autocompletion Setup for cmux

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/README.md

Provides specific instructions for setting up Bash autocompletion for the cmux devbox CLI. This includes sourcing the completion script directly or saving it to a system-wide completion directory.

```bash
source <(cmux completion bash)
```

```bash
cmux completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/cmux
```

```bash
cmux completion bash > $(brew --prefix)/etc/bash_completion.d/cmux
```

--------------------------------

### Run Development Server (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/www/README.md

Commands to start the Next.js development server using different package managers (npm, yarn, pnpm, bun). This allows for local development and testing of the application.

```bash
npm run dev
# or
yarn dev
# or
pnpm dev
# or
bun dev
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Python Script (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/scripts/hello-morph/docker-buildkit-example/README.md

This snippet executes the docker-buildkit_setup.py script using the bash interpreter. It assumes the script has already been made executable.

```bash
./docker-buildkit_setup.py
```

--------------------------------

### Control Morph Cloud VM Lifecycle

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Commands to manage the Morph Cloud VM, including starting it from a base or saved snapshot, stopping it (optionally saving state), checking its status, saving snapshots, and opening the VNC viewer.

```bash
# Start from base snapshot
cmux computer start -w $WS_ID

# Start from a saved snapshot
cmux computer start --from=logged-in -w $WS_ID

# Start from specific snapshot ID
cmux computer start --snapshot=snap_abc123 -w $WS_ID

# Stop the VM
cmux computer stop -w $WS_ID

# Save snapshot before stopping
cmux computer stop --save=my-state -w $WS_ID

# Show VM status and URLs
cmux computer status -w $WS_ID
cmux computer status --json -w $WS_ID

# Save current state as a snapshot
cmux computer save --name=after-login -w $WS_ID

# Open VNC viewer in browser
cmux computer vnc -w $WS_ID
```

--------------------------------

### PowerShell Autocompletion Setup for cmux

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/README.md

Provides the command to set up autocompletion for the cmux devbox CLI in PowerShell. This involves invoking the generated completion script.

```powershell
cmux completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression
```

--------------------------------

### List Accessible GitHub Repositories API

Source: https://context7.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/llms.txt

Lists repositories accessible through a GitHub App installation for a specific team. This GET request requires team and installation ID, and supports search and pagination.

```typescript
// GET /api/integrations/github/repos?team=my-team&installationId=12345&search=api&page=1
const response = await fetch(
  '/api/integrations/github/repos?team=my-team&installationId=12345&search=api',
  { headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${accessToken}` } }
);

// Response
{
  "repos": [
    {
      "name": "api-service",
      "full_name": "owner/api-service",
      "private": true,
      "updated_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      "pushed_at": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```

--------------------------------

### Common Workflow: Create and Access VM with cmux

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/npm/cmux/AGENTS.md

Example workflow demonstrating how to create a new VM, sync a local project directory, and then open VS Code within the newly created VM.

```bash
cmux start ./my-project    # Creates VM, syncs directory, returns ID
cmux code cmux_abc123      # Opens VS Code
```

--------------------------------

### GET /api/integrations/github/repos

Source: https://context7.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/llms.txt

Retrieves a list of GitHub repositories accessible to a specific team via an installed GitHub App.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/integrations/github/repos

### Description
Lists repositories accessible through a GitHub App installation for a team.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/integrations/github/repos

### Query Parameters
- **team** (string) - Required - Team slug.
- **installationId** (string) - Required - GitHub App installation ID.
- **search** (string) - Optional - Search term for filtering repos.
- **page** (number) - Optional - Pagination page number.
```

--------------------------------

### Best Practices for AI Agents: Using Waits

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Shows how to use wait commands to handle dynamic content loading, ensuring subsequent actions are performed only after the content is ready.

```bash
cmux computer click @e1 -w $WS_ID
cmux computer wait --text "Loading complete" -w $WS_ID
cmux computer snapshot -i -w $WS_ID  # Now get fresh refs
```

--------------------------------

### CloudRouter Browser Automation: Snapshot and Navigation

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cloudrouter/README.md

Provides examples of using CloudRouter's browser automation features to get the accessibility tree of a page, open URLs, and navigate within the sandbox browser.

```bash
# Get the accessibility tree with element refs
cloudrouter browser snapshot cr_abc123
# → @e1 [link] About  @e2 [input] Search  @e3 [button] Submit

# Interactive elements only
cloudrouter browser snapshot -i cr_abc123

# Open a URL in the sandbox browser
cloudrouter browser open cr_abc123 "https://example.com"

# Navigate back, forward, reload, or close tab
cloudrouter browser back <id>
cloudrouter browser forward <id>
cloudrouter browser reload <id>
cloudrouter browser close <id>
```

--------------------------------

### Best Practices for AI Agents: Saving Snapshots

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Explains how to save the current state of the VM as a snapshot, allowing for resuming from a specific checkpoint if issues arise.

```bash
# After complex setup
cmux computer save --name=setup-complete -w $WS_ID

# If something goes wrong, resume from checkpoint
cmux computer start --from=setup-complete -w $WS_ID
```

--------------------------------

### Best Practices for AI Agents: Taking Screenshots

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Demonstrates how to take screenshots at different stages of an automated process for debugging and visual verification.

```bash
cmux computer screenshot --output=/tmp/before.png -w $WS_ID
cmux computer click @e1 -w $WS_ID
cmux computer screenshot --output=/tmp/after.png -w $WS_ID
```

--------------------------------

### Install Manaflow CLI using Bun or npm

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/README.md

This snippet shows how to install the Manaflow CLI (cmux) using either Bun or npm package managers. It demonstrates both direct execution and global installation.

```bash
# with bun
bunx cmux@latest

# with npm
npx cmux@latest

# or to install globally
bun add -g cmux@latest
npm install -g cmux@latest
```

--------------------------------

### cmux CLI Tips and Flags

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/skills/cmux/SKILL.md

General tips for using the cmux CLI effectively. Includes authentication, output formatting, team selection, verbosity, and best practices for browser automation.

```bash
# Run `cmux login` first if not authenticated
# Use `--json` flag for machine-readable output
# Use `-t <team>` to override default team
# Use `-v` for verbose output
# Always run `snapshot` first to see available elements before browser automation
# Use element refs (`@e1`) for reliability over CSS selectors
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshooting: Page Loads But No Elements Found

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Provides solutions for scenarios where a page loads but no elements are detected, suggesting waits for content or specific text.

```bash
# Wait for page to fully load
cmux computer wait 3000 -w $WS_ID
cmux computer snapshot -i -w $WS_ID

# Or wait for specific content
cmux computer wait --text "Welcome" -w $WS_ID
cmux computer snapshot -i -w $WS_ID
```

--------------------------------

### Install CloudRouter CLI

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cloudrouter/README.md

Installs the CloudRouter command-line interface globally using npm. This allows you to use CloudRouter commands directly from your terminal.

```bash
npm install -g @manaflow-ai/cloudrouter
cloudrouter login
```

--------------------------------

### General CLI Tips

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/skills/cmux/SKILL.md

Useful tips and flags for using the Manaflow AI CLI effectively.

```APIDOC
## General CLI Tips

### Description
A collection of useful tips and command-line flags to enhance your experience with the Manaflow AI CLI.

### Tips
- Run `cmux login` first if you are not authenticated.
- Use the `--json` flag for machine-readable output, which is helpful for scripting.
- Use the `-t <team>` flag to specify a different team if you are part of multiple teams.
- Use the `-v` flag for verbose output, which provides more detailed information about command execution.
- Always run `snapshot` before performing browser automation to identify available elements reliably.
- Prefer using element references (e.g., `@e1`) over CSS selectors for greater stability in automation scripts.
```

--------------------------------

### Install Zod OpenAPI Hono and Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/docs-ai/hono-openapi.md

Installs the Zod OpenAPI Hono package along with hono and zod. These are the core dependencies required for using the library.

```sh
npm i hono zod @hono/zod-openapi
```

--------------------------------

### Start and Execute Commands on a New VM

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/npm/cmux/llms.txt

Demonstrates creating a new VM, executing development commands within it, and then opening VS Code for further work. This showcases the basic workflow of provisioning and interacting with a development environment.

```bash
cmux start ./my-app
cmux exec cmux_abc123 "npm install && npm run dev"
cmux code cmux_abc123
```

--------------------------------

### Verify cmux devbox CLI installation

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/internal/README.md

Verifies that the cmux devbox CLI has been successfully installed and is accessible from the command line by checking its help message and version.

```bash
cmux --help
cmux version
```

--------------------------------

### Browser Automation Workflow: Login

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/skills/cmux/SKILL.md

A step-by-step example of automating a website login process using cmux. It involves opening a URL, taking a snapshot to identify elements, filling input fields, and clicking the submit button. Outputs a screenshot upon completion.

```bash
cmux computer open cmux_abc123 "https://example.com/login"
cmux computer snapshot cmux_abc123
# Output: @e1 [input] Email, @e2 [input] Password, @e3 [button] Sign In

cmux computer fill cmux_abc123 @e1 "user@example.com"
cmux computer fill cmux_abc123 @e2 "password123"
cmux computer click cmux_abc123 @e3
cmux computer screenshot cmux_abc123 result.png
```

--------------------------------

### Install CloudRouter as an Agent Skill

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cloudrouter/README.md

Installs CloudRouter as a skill for coding agents like Claude Code or Codex. This enables agents to leverage CloudRouter's capabilities within their workflows.

```bash
npx skills add manaflow-ai/cloudrouter
```

--------------------------------

### Install Manaflow CLI using uv

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/README.md

This command installs the Manaflow CLI (cmux) using the 'uvx' command, a tool often associated with Python package management.

```bash
uvx cmux@latest
```

--------------------------------

### Display cmux help information

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux/README.md

Shows all available command-line options and usage instructions for cmux. This is useful for understanding the full range of configurations and functionalities.

```bash
cmux --help
```

--------------------------------

### Start Manaflow AI Daemon

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

This command starts the background daemon responsible for managing workspace state and providing Inter-Process Communication (IPC) for the Manaflow AI CLI. The output includes the process ID (PID), the socket path, and the status of the daemon.

```bash
cmux daemon start
# Output: {"pid": 12345, "socket": "/Users/.../.cmux/daemon.sock", "status": "started"}
```

--------------------------------

### GET /api/environments

Source: https://context7.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/llms.txt

Retrieves all environments configured for a specific team.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/environments

### Description
Retrieves all environments configured for a team.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/environments

### Query Parameters
- **teamSlugOrId** (string) - Required - The team identifier.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **environments** (array) - List of environment objects.

#### Response Example
[
  {
    "id": "env_abc123",
    "name": "Production Environment",
    "morphSnapshotId": "snap_xyz789"
  }
]
```

--------------------------------

### Create and Sync a Manaflow AI VM

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/README.md

Demonstrates how to create a new virtual machine (VM) using Manaflow AI. It also shows how to optionally synchronize a local directory with the VM.

```bash
cmux start                       # Create VM (no sync)
cmux start .                     # Create VM, sync current directory
cmux start ./my-project          # Create VM, sync specific directory
cmux start --snapshot=snap_xxx   # Create from specific snapshot
```

--------------------------------

### Troubleshooting: Browser Commands Fail

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Offers solutions for when browser commands fail, such as checking the VM status and attempting to reconnect.

```bash
# Check VM is running
cmux computer status -w $WS_ID
# Must show "Status: running"

# If commands timeout, try reconnecting
cmux computer status -w $WS_ID
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Management Commands

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/skills/cmux/SKILL.md

Commands for starting, stopping, deleting, and interacting with sandboxes.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Management Commands

### Description
Commands for managing sandboxes, including starting new ones, opening development environments, and accessing terminals.

### Method
CLI Commands

### Endpoints
`cmux <command> [arguments]`

### Parameters
#### Command Arguments
- **start** (string) - Required - Creates a new sandbox and uploads project files from the specified local directory.
- **code** (string) - Required - Opens VS Code within the specified sandbox.
- **pty** (string) - Required - Opens an interactive terminal session within the specified sandbox.
- **stop** (string) - Required - Stops the specified sandbox. The sandbox can be restarted later.
- **delete** (string) - Required - Permanently deletes the specified sandbox.
- **ls** - Lists available sandboxes.

### Request Example
```bash
cmux start ./my-project
cmux code cmux_abc123
cmux pty cmux_abc123
cmux stop cmux_abc123
cmux delete cmux_abc123
cmux ls
```

### Response
#### Success Response
Output varies. `start` may show upload progress. `code` and `pty` will provide URLs or initiate the respective applications. `stop` and `delete` may provide confirmation.

#### Response Example
```
✓ Sandbox created and project uploaded.
Opening VS Code in sandbox cmux_abc123...
```
```

--------------------------------

### Common Workflows

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/npm/cmux/AGENTS.md

Illustrative examples of common tasks performed using the cmux CLI.

```APIDOC
## Common Workflows

### Description
Examples demonstrating how to accomplish frequent tasks using the cmux CLI.

### Workflow Examples

#### Create and Access a VM
```bash
cmux start ./my-project    # Creates VM, syncs directory, returns ID
cmux code cmux_abc123      # Opens VS Code in the browser
```

#### Run Commands Remotely
```bash
cmux exec cmux_abc123 "npm install"
cmux exec cmux_abc123 "npm run dev"
```

#### Sync Files
```bash
cmux sync cmux_abc123 .              # Push current directory to VM
cmux sync cmux_abc123 ./dist --pull  # Pull build output from VM
```

#### Browser Automation
```bash
cmux computer open cmux_abc123 "https://localhost:3000"
cmux computer snapshot cmux_abc123   # View clickable elements
cmux computer click cmux_abc123 @e1  # Click the first identified element
```

#### End of Session
```bash
cmux pause cmux_abc123    # Pause VM to save costs (can be resumed later)
# OR
cmux delete cmux_abc123   # Permanently delete the VM
```
```

--------------------------------

### Zsh Shell Integration

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cmux-devbox/README.md

Instructions for integrating cmux with the Zsh shell for autocompletion and setup.

```APIDOC
## Zsh Shell Integration

### Description
Integrate cmux with your Zsh shell for enhanced command-line experience, including autocompletion.

### Setup

1. **Enable Completion**: Add the following line to your `~/.zshrc` file:
   ```bash
   autoload -U compinit; compinit
   ```

2. **Load cmux Completion**: Source the completion script for cmux.
   ```bash
   source <(cmux completion zsh)
   ```

3. **Install Completion Script**: To make completion persistent, copy the completion script to your Zsh site-functions directory:
   ```bash
   cmux completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_cmux"
   ```
   Alternatively, if using Homebrew, you can install it to the Homebrew prefix:
   ```bash
   cmux completion zsh > $(brew --prefix)/share/zsh/site-functions/_cmux
   ```
```

--------------------------------

### Best Practices for AI Agents: Verifying Actions

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Illustrates how to verify that an action, such as filling a form field, has been successfully completed by retrieving the field's value.

```bash
cmux computer fill @e2 "test@example.com" -w $WS_ID
cmux computer get value @e2 -w $WS_ID  # Verify it was filled
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Management

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/packages/cloudrouter/README.md

Commands for managing CloudRouter sandbox environments, including starting, stopping, listing, and checking their status.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Management

### Start a new sandbox

- **Method**: `POST` (conceptual, actual command is `cloudrouter start`)
- **Endpoint**: `/sandboxes`
- **Description**: Creates and starts a new sandbox environment.
- **Parameters**:
  - **Query Parameters**:
    - `.` (string) - Optional - If provided, initializes the sandbox from the current directory.
  - **Options**:
    - `--gpu <type>` - Specifies the type of GPU to allocate (e.g., 'T4', 'B200').
    - `--size <preset>` - Specifies a size preset for the sandbox (e.g., 'small', 'medium', 'large').

### List running sandboxes

- **Method**: `GET` (conceptual, actual command is `cloudrouter ls`)
- **Endpoint**: `/sandboxes`
- **Description**: Lists all currently running sandbox environments.

### Check sandbox status

- **Method**: `GET` (conceptual, actual command is `cloudrouter status`)
- **Endpoint**: `/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/status`
- **Description**: Retrieves the status of a specific sandbox.
- **Parameters**:
  - **Path Parameters**:
    - `sandbox_id` (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox to check.

### Extend sandbox timeout

- **Method**: `POST` (conceptual, actual command is `cloudrouter extend`)
- **Endpoint**: `/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/extend`
- **Description**: Extends the timeout period for a running sandbox.
- **Parameters**:
  - **Path Parameters**:
    - `sandbox_id` (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox to extend.

### Stop a sandbox

- **Method**: `DELETE` (conceptual, actual command is `cloudrouter stop`)
- **Endpoint**: `/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}`
- **Description**: Stops a running sandbox environment.
- **Parameters**:
  - **Path Parameters**:
    - `sandbox_id` (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox to stop.

### Resume a paused sandbox

- **Method**: `POST` (conceptual, actual command is `cloudrouter resume`)
- **Endpoint**: `/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}/resume`
- **Description**: Resumes a sandbox that was previously paused.
- **Parameters**:
  - **Path Parameters**:
    - `sandbox_id` (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox to resume.

### Delete a sandbox

- **Method**: `DELETE` (conceptual, actual command is `cloudrouter delete`)
- **Endpoint**: `/sandboxes/{sandbox_id}`
- **Description**: Deletes a sandbox environment permanently.
- **Parameters**:
  - **Path Parameters**:
    - `sandbox_id` (string) - Required - The ID of the sandbox to delete.
```

--------------------------------

### Run Local Inject Script with Inline Brackets Strategy (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/www/scripts/pr-review/README.md

Tests the local Docker runner for the PR review inject script using inline bracket highlights. Requires bun, a PR URL, and specific diff flags.

```bash
bun run apps/www/scripts/pr-review-local.ts \
  --strategy inline-brackets \
  --diff-line-numbers \
  --diff-context-line-numbers \
  --diff-artifact single \
  <PR_URL>
```

--------------------------------

### GET /sandboxes/{id}/attach

Source: https://context7.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/llms.txt

Establishes a WebSocket connection to a sandbox to provide an interactive terminal session.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandboxes/{id}/attach

### Description
Establishes an interactive terminal session with a sandbox via WebSocket.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandboxes/{id}/attach

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **cols** (integer) - Optional - Number of terminal columns
- **rows** (integer) - Optional - Number of terminal rows
- **tty** (boolean) - Optional - Enable TTY mode

### Request Example
ws://localhost:46835/sandboxes/dev-sandbox/attach?cols=120&rows=40&tty=true

### Response
#### Success Response (101 Switching Protocols)
- **stream** (WebSocket) - Interactive terminal data stream
```

--------------------------------

### Run Unit Tests with Go

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/apps/devbox/README.md

Executes unit tests for Go projects. Supports running all tests, tests for specific packages, and generating coverage reports.

```bash
cd dba # Note: the package directory is still named 'dba'

# Run all unit tests
go test ./... -v

# Run tests for a specific package
go test ./internal/morph/... -v
go test ./internal/browser/... -v

# Run with coverage
go test ./... -coverprofile=coverage.out
go tool cover -html=coverage.out -o coverage.html
```

--------------------------------

### Start a Sandbox Environment

Source: https://context7.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/llms.txt

Creates and provisions a new sandbox instance. This can include cloning a repository, setting environment variables, and executing maintenance scripts. It also supports warm pool instances for quicker startup times.

```APIDOC
## POST /api/sandboxes/start

### Description
Creates and provisions a new sandbox instance with optional repository cloning, environment variables, and maintenance scripts. Supports warm pool instances for faster startup times.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/api/sandboxes/start

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
- **teamSlugOrId** (string) - Required - The team identifier.
- **repoUrl** (string) - Required - The URL of the repository to clone.
- **branch** (string) - Optional - The branch to clone from.
- **newBranch** (string) - Optional - The name for a new branch to create.
- **ttlSeconds** (integer) - Optional - Time-to-live for the sandbox in seconds.
- **taskRunId** (string) - Optional - Identifier for the task run.
- **taskRunJwt** (string) - Optional - JWT for task run authentication.
- **isCloudWorkspace** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to create a cloud workspace.
- **depth** (integer) - Optional - Git clone depth.
- **environmentId** (string) - Optional - ID of a pre-configured environment.
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Additional metadata for the sandbox.
  - **purpose** (string) - Optional - The purpose of the sandbox.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "teamSlugOrId": "my-team",
  "repoUrl": "https://github.com/owner/repo",
  "branch": "main",
  "newBranch": "feature/new-feature",
  "ttlSeconds": 3600,
  "taskRunId": "task_abc123",
  "taskRunJwt": "jwt-token-here",
  "isCloudWorkspace": true,
  "depth": 1,
  "environmentId": "env_xyz789",
  "metadata": {
    "purpose": "feature-development"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **instanceId** (string) - The unique identifier for the provisioned sandbox instance.
- **vscodeUrl** (string) - The URL to access the VS Code workspace.
- **workerUrl** (string) - The URL for the sandbox worker.
- **provider** (string) - The provider of the sandbox (e.g., "morph").
- **vscodePersisted** (boolean) - Indicates if the VS Code workspace is persisted.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "instanceId": "morphvm_abc123",
  "vscodeUrl": "https://port-39378-morphvm-abc123.http.cloud.morph.so",
  "workerUrl": "https://port-39377-morphvm-abc123.http.cloud.morph.so",
  "provider": "morph",
  "vscodePersisted": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Get Sandbox Status

Source: https://context7.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/llms.txt

Retrieves the current operational status and relevant URLs for a specified sandbox instance.

```APIDOC
## GET /api/sandboxes/{id}/status

### Description
Retrieves the current status and URLs for a running sandbox instance.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/api/sandboxes/{id}/status

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **id** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the sandbox instance.

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
None

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **running** (boolean) - Indicates if the sandbox is currently running.
- **vscodeUrl** (string) - The URL to access the VS Code workspace if running.
- **workerUrl** (string) - The URL for the sandbox worker if running.
- **provider** (string) - The provider of the sandbox (e.g., "morph").

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "running": true,
  "vscodeUrl": "https://port-39378-morphvm-abc123.http.cloud.morph.so",
  "workerUrl": "https://port-39377-morphvm-abc123.http.cloud.morph.so",
  "provider": "morph"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Upgrade Manaflow CLI

Source: https://github.com/manaflow-ai/manaflow/blob/main/README.md

This command is used to upgrade an existing installation of the Manaflow CLI (cmux) to the latest version.

```bash
cmux upgrade
```
